title: Zambia : There is a restraining court order against Tullow Zambia's Oil and Gas Exploration-GEARS
impact_evidence: Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Mining permitting and licensing practices -> "the Court directed Tullow Zambia BV to comply with the Order forthwith stopping its engagement in Petroleum Exploration in Block 31." | "an Ex-Parte Order from the High Court of Zambia restraining Tullow Zambia BV from "undertaking or engaging in any petroleum exploration activities of whatsoever nature and description with respect to the area particularly described and known as block 31 located in the Northern Province of Zambia."" || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Legislative and regulatory framework effectiveness -> "Therefore, President Edgar Lungu's launch and commissioning of the Tullow Oil and Petroleum Exploration Project in Kasama district of Northern on Friday, August 11, 2017, unless it is not part of the specified Block 31 in the Court Order, is a blatant disregard of the Ex-Parte Order from the Court awarded to the Appellant and undermines the doctrine of the Separation of Power and rule of law in this country." | "If President Lungu acted in knowledge of this Ex-Parte Order and decided to disregard its orders and directives, then Zambia's judiciary and the doctrine of Separation of State Power is under siege from the Executive." || Governance | Transparency & Accountability | Corporate transparency and reporting -> "The Attorney General, Likando Kalaluka, S.C, should inform the nation the exact legal status of Tullow Zambia's Oil and Gas Exploration Project in Kasama District of Northern Province." || Governance | Reputation & Social License | Mining image and public perception -> "Good governance is an imperative of sound democratic process and also investor confidence. Disregarding court orders is bad governance and undermines the growth and existence of an enviable democratic process in any country."
